account_plan_attestation: Starter paid subscription, verified through the account API on 2026-07-14
commercial_use_basis: generated during the account owner's paid Starter subscription; use is subject to the ElevenLabs terms captured by the raw candidate metadata

cues.prompt: One isolated medieval arcade menu move: a tiny hardwood lever click followed by two very short bright hammered-metal notes. Tactile, friendly, dry close studio recording. Exactly one compact cue. No music bed, no ambience, no voice, no retro game imitation, no long reverb.

One isolated realistic giant bird wing flap carrying an armored rider: a powerful close feather sweep with a compact low air-pressure thump and faint harness movement. Exactly one downstroke, fast natural decay. No second flap, no bird call, no whoosh sequence, no music, no voice, no cinematic boom.

One isolated realistic giant bird footstep on a stone platform: hard claw tips and a padded foot plant once, with a tiny rider-armor jingle. Dry close Foley, quick and light. Exactly one step. No walking sequence, no bird voice, no music, no ambience, no bass boom.

One isolated realistic landing by a large armored bird onto a stone platform: two clawed feet hit together with a heavy body thud, brief claw scrape, leather tack movement, and small armor clink. Close dry Foley, one landing only. No wing flap, no bird call, no music, no voice, no cinematic sub-bass.

One isolated high-speed medieval joust collision: two steel-armored riders strike lances, producing a hard steel impact, one sharp wooden lance splinter, and a compact body collision. Close dry action Foley, forceful but natural. Exactly one collision. No explosion, no repeated hits, no crowd, no voices, no music, no long hall reverb.

One isolated fantasy giant bird taking a hit and tumbling in armor: a short natural nonverbal squawk, immediate feather and leather movement, one compact metal rattle, and a small body thud. Original creature Foley, readable and brief. No human speech, no words, no repeated impacts, no music, no ambience, no long reverb.

One isolated large fantasy bird egg settling onto stone: a gentle hollow shell knock, two tiny ceramic shell ticks, and a very short natural resonance. Delicate close Foley, intact egg, one event. No hatching, no creature call, no magical music, no voice, no ambience, no long reverb.

One isolated fantasy bird egg hatching quickly: three crisp eggshell cracks, small shell fragments scattering on stone, then one tiny original hatchling chirp. Close dry Foley and natural creature sound, compact sequence. No human voice, no repeated chirping, no music, no ambience, no long reverb.

A very short original medieval new-wave fanfare played by two real herald trumpets: four clear rising notes and one confident final accent, immediate attack and clean ending. Isolated studio recording, energetic and compact. No drums, no orchestra, no crowd, no speech, no battle noise, no synthesizer, no long reverb.

source_recordings.note: molten roar bed, slowed/darkened; borrowed from fire_sound_generator

mastering.description: one-shots use onset trim, exact duration fit, equal-power mono downmix, DC removal, 24 Hz high-pass, short edge fades, and 4x reconstructed-peak gain staging; loops use stable-window selection, equal-value smoothstep boundary preparation, exact duration fit, and reconstructed-peak gain staging

clap.purpose: candidate triage only; not a substitute for listening
clap.reproducibility: normalized candidate score arrays identical across two independent runs

selection.pilot_policy: retain any user-auditioned pilot candidates recorded for this pack
selection.quality_priority: new packs rank semantic and signal validity ahead of waveform diversity; correlation remains recorded for audit

sfx_sublicensing_opt_out: not observable through the API; managed with the Disable control on the ElevenLabs Sound Effects product page
standalone_distribution: intended only as bundled joustix game assets, not a standalone SFX library
locally_generated_note: As of 2026-07-16 the lava cue (x4) is locally generated by lava_sound_generator from a CC0 recording (PagDev Fireplace Sound loop, CC0) plus procedural bubbles, replacing the ElevenLabs renders (archived under ~/research/gpu_terminal/sound/older_assets/joustix/elevenlabs_lava_2026-07-16/). The remaining cues are still ElevenLabs; provider/model/terms below describe those.
